evals.expected_output: Full autonomous implementation through all 10 phases: story fetch, TDD, implementation, UI integration, DHH review, visual verification, quality gates, PR creation, documentation, and final review
expectations: Phase 1: Fetches the story from SmartSuite and creates a git worktree with feature branch
Phase 1: Updates SmartSuite story status to in-progress
Phase 2: Writes failing tests FIRST before any implementation code (TDD red phase)
Phase 2: Tests cover the core acceptance criteria from the user story
Phase 3: Implements code to make the failing tests pass (TDD green phase)
Phase 3: All tests pass after implementation
Phase 4: New pages and features are reachable from the UI (not orphaned routes)
Phase 5: Invokes dhh-code-reviewer agent for iterative code review
Phase 5: Auto-applies Critical and Major fixes from the review
Phase 5: Runs multiple review passes until Rails-worthy or max passes reached
Phase 6: Takes screenshots of the implemented feature for documentation
Phase 7: Runs quality gates (bin/rails test, bin/rubocop, bin/brakeman) and all pass
Phase 8: Updates CHANGELOG.md with the new feature entry
Phase 8: Creates a PR with descriptive title and body
Phase 9: Updates documentation if a docs site exists
Phase 9: Updates SmartSuite story status to completed
Phase 10: Runs a final DHH review of ALL changes on the branch (not just latest commit)
Phase 10: The final review uses the full branch diff (git diff main...HEAD)
All 10 phases are recorded in the autopilot state file
The state file is cleaned up after successful completion

evals.expected_output: Fetches the next ready story from SmartSuite backlog and runs full 10-phase autopilot
expectations: Queries SmartSuite for the next backlog story scoped to this project
Reports which story was selected before starting implementation
Completes all 10 phases without user interaction
Creates a PR at the end with all changes
Updates SmartSuite status at start (in-progress) and end (completed)

evals.expected_output: Runs the autopilot using an existing architecture spec instead of fetching from SmartSuite
expectations: Reads the architecture spec from the provided path
Creates a feature branch based on the spec name
Writes tests based on the spec's acceptance criteria (Phase 2)
Implements the feature following the spec's design decisions (Phase 3)
Runs all subsequent phases (4-10) as normal
The implementation follows the architectural decisions in the spec
